title: TC001-Loading spinner and message appear after submitting a valid URL
description: Verifies that submitting a valid article URL shows a visible spinning loader and descriptive loading message while analysis is running.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Type the test article URL into the article URL input (index 3) and submit the form (send Enter) to trigger analysis so the loader and loading message can be observed.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/section/form/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('https://example.com/news/sample-article')
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC004-Error state clears spinner and shows red error box with prefix
description: Verifies that when analysis fails, the spinner is cleared and a red error box with an "Error:" prefix is shown.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Type https://example.com/news/likely-to-fail into the article URL input (index 3). Then report that the Analyze control is missing and finish the test.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/section/form/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('https://example.com/news/likely-to-fail')
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Analyzing...')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Analyzing...' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Error:')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Error:' to be visible"
        assert not await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Analyzing...')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Analyzing...' to not be visible"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Analyze button not found on page
- Unable to perform analysis: could not click Analyze to trigger spinner or error message

title: TC007-Empty URL submission shows validation error banner
description: Verifies that submitting with an empty URL shows a red error box with an "Error:" prefix and a validation message.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Focus the URL input and submit the form with an empty value (simulate pressing Enter) to trigger validation and display the error UI.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/section/form/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Error:')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Error:' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'red error')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'red error' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'URL')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'URL' to be visible"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

- Validation message containing the word 'URL' was not visible on the page.

title: TC015-Partial-data run shows at least one stat card with placeholder or Not available
description: Verifies that when analysis completes with partial data, one or more stat cards show a placeholder or "Not available" instead of a numeric value.
code:

from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Type the provided article URL into the article URL input (index 3) and submit the form to trigger analysis for the current viewport.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/section/form/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('https://www.theguardian.com/international')
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state

        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'analysis loading spinner')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'analysis loading spinner' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Ownership nodes')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Ownership nodes' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Author articles')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Author articles' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Active alerts')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Active alerts' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Not available')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Not available' to be visible"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Type the article URL into the article URL input (index 3) and submit the form (press Enter) to trigger analysis.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/section/form/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('https://www.nytimes.com/2026/02/01/us/politics/example.html')
        
        # -> Click the Analyze button to re-run analysis (click element index 74). Then re-check the three assertions and proceed to mark task done.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/section/form/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC027-Author Panel renders for an article that includes author metadata
description: Verifies Layer 2 appears after analysis and displays populated author details when author metadata exists.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Type 'https://example.com/news/article-with-author' into the article URL input field (index 3) and submit the form (press Enter).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/main/section/form/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('https://example.com/news/article-with-author')
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Loading')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Loading' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Layer 2')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Layer 2' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Author')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Author' to be visible"
        assert (await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Author')]").nth(0).text_content()).strip() != "Author", "Expected 'Author' field value to be visible and not empty"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Coverage pattern')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Coverage pattern' to be visible"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
